rag_rules: Place assumptions in component type annexes when referencing ports or variables defined in the AADL component type.
Ensure all variables in AGREE expressions are explicitly visible in the scope via AADL ports, outputs, or declared constants.
generic_rules: Use only numeric scalars for range comparisons; avoid Boolean or opaque data in arithmetic expressions.
Declare helper boolean variables for complex conditions to ensure type consistency and clarity.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` and placed in component type or implementation scopes as appropriate.
Comparison expressions like x >= min and x <= max must evaluate to bool; use explicit helper declarations if needed.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) directly as variables in AGREE expressions.
Do not convert Boolean or enumeration ports into numeric ranges without explicit scalar representation in the AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
